title: festuca arvernensis subsp. costei
intro: Festuca arvernensis subsp. costei, also known as Coste's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to France and other parts of Europe. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Poaceae and is valued for its medicinal properties. Festuca arvernensis subsp. costei has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in herbal supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
